scenario: Prosecution asks Taylor Durden: 'You didn't care about Jessica's safety; you just wanted to top Alex's record as Pledge Master, didn't you?'
feedback: Attorneys shall not ask argumentative questions that badger the witness[cite: 1].

scenario: Sgt. Knight testifies: 'Carmen Cordova told me she quit the game because her professor said drinking that much water was harmful.'
feedback: This is an out-of-court statement offered for the truth of the matter[cite: 4, 10].

scenario: Shawn Boyd testifies: 'Jessica was just acting like a clown to get attention when she collapsed.'
feedback: A witness may not testify to matters unless they have personal knowledge. [cite_start]Boyd cannot know Jessica's internal intent[cite: 8].

scenario: Defense asks Sgt. Knight: 'Is it true you were kicked out of ESE because a member accused you of cheating on finals?'
feedback: Evidence of a person's character trait is generally not admissible to prove they acted in accordance with it[cite: 8].

scenario: Dr. Chessler testifies: 'Dr. Jackson is only here because he makes $200,000 a year as a hired gun.'
feedback: The probative value of the 'hired gun' label is substantially outweighed by the danger of unfair prejudice[cite: 8].
